An example Hyperband schedule is given in @tbl-hyperband where $s = 3$ is the
most exploratory bracket and $s = 0$ essentially performs a random search using
the full budget. @tbl-hyperband-eg demonstrates how this schedule may look if we
were to tune 20 different hyperparameter configurations; note that each entry in
the table is a unique ID referring to a possible configuration of multiple
hyperparameters to tune.

+-----+-------------------+-------------------+
|     | $s = 3$           | $s = 2$           |
+-----+-------------------+-------------------+
| $i$ | $m_{i}$           | $r_{i}$           |
+=====+===================+===================+
| 0   | 8                 | 1                 |
+-----+-------------------+-------------------+
| 1   | 4                 | 2                 |
+-----+-------------------+-------------------+

: Hyperband schedule with the number of configurations, $m_{i}$, and
  resources, $r_{i}$, for each bracket, $s$, and stage, $i$, when $\eta = 2$,
  $r{_{min}} = 1$ and $r{_{max}} = 8$. {#tbl-hyperband}

|         | $s = 3$          | $s = 2$             |
| ------- | ---------------- | ------------------- |
| $i = 0$ | $\{1, 2, 3, 4\}$ | $\{9, 10, 11, 12\}$ |
| $i = 1$ | $\{1, 2\}$       | $\{9, 14\}$         |

: Hyperparameter configurations in each stage and bracket from the schedule in
  @tbl-hyperband. Entries are unique identifiers for tested hyperparameter
  configurations (HPCs). $HPC^*_s$ is the optimal hyperparameter configuration
  in bracket $s$ and $HPC^*$ is the optimal hyperparameter configuration
  across all brackets. {#tbl-hyperband-eg}
